title: Podcast EP351: A Detailed Overview of the Emerging Standards for 400G with Kent Lusted - Semiwiki
gist: Podcast show-notes for a Daniel Nenni interview with Synopsys Distinguished Architect Kent Lusted on the early-stage IEEE 400G Ethernet signaling standards effort, which Lusted says will be critical for emerging scale-up and scale-out AI workloads.

insights.claim: The 400G Ethernet signaling standards effort is in its very early stages, and the prior 200G standards work is still not complete.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Early-stage 400G (with 200G unfinished) signals a multi-year standards runway; relevant for timing IP and PHY vendors' product cycles.
insights.quote: the prior 200G standards work is still not complete, so the 400G effort is indeed in its early stages.
insights.locator: The 400G standards effort is in its very early stages.

insights.claim: Kent Lusted argues 400G speeds will be critical for the requirements of emerging scale-up and scale-out AI workloads.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Ties 400G Ethernet demand directly to AI cluster scaling; Synopsys Ethernet IP is positioned to benefit as designs move toward 400G.
insights.quote: 400G speeds will be critical for the requirements of emerging scale-up and scale-out AI workloads.
insights.locator: That said, he points out that 400G speeds will be critical...
